# zer0dex

> Dual-layer memory architecture for persistent AI agents. Compressed markdown index + local vector store. 91.2% recall, 70ms latency, $0/month.

zer0dex gives AI agents long-term memory by combining two layers:
1. A compressed markdown index (always in context, ~782 tokens) — acts as a semantic table of contents
2. A local vector store (mem0 + chromadb) — queried automatically on every inbound message via HTTP hook

The dual-layer approach outperforms both flat files alone (52.2%) and standard RAG alone (80.3%). On cross-reference queries, zer0dex scores 80% vs 37.5% for full RAG — the compressed index provides navigational scaffolding that helps bridge domains vector similarity can't connect.

Stack: Python 3.11+, mem0ai, chromadb, ollama (nomic-embed-text + mistral:7b). Fully local, no external API calls.

## Docs
- [README](README.md): Architecture overview, benchmarks, quick start, comparison table
- [Evaluation Methodology](eval/README.md): How the n=97 benchmark works, scoring, limitations

## Code
- [Memory Server](src/zer0dex/server.py): HTTP server for memory queries (GET /health, POST /query, POST /add)
- [CLI](src/zer0dex/cli.py): Command-line interface (check, init, seed, serve, query, status, add)
- [Seeder](src/zer0dex/seed.py): Seeds markdown files into the vector store
- [Hook Example](src/zer0dex/hook_example.ts): TypeScript integration pattern for agent frameworks

## Evaluation
- [Full Benchmark](eval/evaluate.py): n=97 test suite across direct recall, cross-reference, and negative queries
- [Quick Benchmark](eval/evaluate_small.py): n=10 subset for fast iteration

## Optional
- [License](LICENSE): Apache 2.0
